channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New EPA guidance says spending items greater than $50,000 must get approval from DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency has issued new guidance directing that spending items greater than $50,000 now require approval from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Compounders must stop making copies of Eli Lilly's weight loss drugs: Judge
item.description: Compounders had been allowed to produce and sell hundreds of thousands of their own versions of popular anti-obesity drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Masked home invader 'shot' after 'pistol-whipping' OnlyFans star, demanding cryptocurrency
item.description: OnlyFans model Kaitlyn “Amouranth" Siragusa provided real-time updates to her followers as armed intruders stormed her Texas home and demanded access to her cryptocurrency accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Bragg to Liberty and back again: Ceremony to rechristen Army post once named for a Confederate
item.description: FORT BRAGG, N.C. (AP) — The short-lived existence of Fort Liberty is set to an end Friday when the nation’s largest Army installation officially returns to its former name: Fort Bragg. Christened a century ago in honor of Confederate Gen.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN BREED and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 12 indicted migrants allegedly assaulted officers at South Texas ICE lockup
item.description: A federal grand jury in South Texas has indicted 10 Mexican nationals and two men from Guatemala on charges of assaulting two correctional officers, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gavin Newsom splits with Dems on trans athletes, prompting backlash from LGBTQ+ advocates
item.description: In a rebuke against the Democratic Party, California Gov. Gavin Newsom has revealed his thoughts on transgender athletes competing in girls' sporting events, and it has spurred reactions from across the state.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: US foreign aid cuts leave a funding gap that private donors are unlikely to fill
item.description: Charitable organizations that relied on U.S. foreign assistance are looking to different private and public donors now that the Trump administration suspended nearly all foreign aid contracts. Past giving patterns suggest such a lifeline is unlikely.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thrust into unemployment, axed federal workers face relatives who celebrate their firing
item.description: Scrambling to replace their health insurance and to find new work, some fired federal workers are running into another unexpected unpleasantry: Relatives cheering their job loss.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why are clocks set forward in the spring? Thank wars, confusion and a hunger for sunlight
item.description: Once again, most Americans will set their clocks forward by one hour this weekend, losing perhaps a bit of sleep but gaining more glorious sunlight in the evenings as the days warm into summer.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that killed seven people when it plummeted into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January and likely had not been functioning for several years, the National Transportation Safety Board said in a preliminary rep
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, KRON4

item.title: 2 men plead not guilty in drug overdose deaths of 3 Kansas City Chiefs fans after game
item.description: PLATTE CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two men pleaded not guilty Thursday in the drug overdose deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans whose bodies were found in a backyard two days after they got together to watch the final game of the regular season in 2024.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk's PAC is running ads touting Trump's accomplishments
item.description: The political action committee that Elon Musk funded to the tune of more than $200 million on behalf of President Donald Trump launched a television advertisement this week lauding the first weeks of the administration in which the Tesla CEO is serving.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job” and close the Education Department. McMahon was confirmed by the Senate on Monday, and an executive order to shutter the department could come as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Police identify 2 people who died in Feb. 19 midair collision at southern Arizona airport
item.description: MARANA, Ariz. (AP) — Authorities have publicly identified two people killed two weeks ago in the midair collision of two small planes at a southern Arizona airport. Police say 70-year-old Michael Reinath and 76-year-old Linda Gifford died in the Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: California company celebrates successful return of medical research space capsule
item.description: A Southern California company is celebrating the successful launch and return of a space capsule designed to change how pharmaceuticals are processed in Earth's low orbit. Varda Space Industries, based in El Segundo, launched its W-2 capsule aboard a Space X rocket in mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man threatens to assassinate Trump, destroy New York with missiles in 911 calls: report
item.description: Justin Blaxton, 34, allegedly threatened to assassinate President Donald Trump and launch missiles to destroy New York in a series of 911 calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pamela Bach, actor and ex-wife of David Hasselhoff, dies at 62
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pamela Bach, an actor and the ex-wife of “Baywatch” star David Hasselhoff, has died at 62. The Los Angeles medical examiner's office reports that she died Wednesday and the cause is still under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Second judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s sweeping OMB funding freeze
item.description: A second federal judge indefinitely blocked President Trump’s blanket freeze on federal grants and loans, saying the administration “put itself above Congress.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Abbey Gate terror suspect's mugshot revealed as he makes first federal court appearance
item.description: Police in Virginia released a mugshot of Mohammad Sharifullah, the suspect who allegedly scouted the attack route for the Abbey Gate bombing in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WGN 9

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger's defense claims he has autism in bid to avoid firing squad
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's autism diagnosis should take the death penalty off the table if he is convicted of the Idaho student murders, defense lawyer argues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denver mayor grilled over area’s Tren de Aragua problem as GOP lawmaker says policies to blame
item.description: Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston is taking heat from state lawmakers over "weak immigration laws" and faced Congress over sanctuary status this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is unwilling to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs on the United States if President Donald Trump leaves any U.S. tariffs on Canada, a senior government official told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ash Wednesday: What you should know about the start of the season
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) – What is Ash Wednesday? Ash Wednesday 2025, which falls on March 5 this year, marks the official beginning of the Christian season of Lent, which culminates with Easter on April 20, 2025. The 46-day season is meant for penitence, sacrifice, and reflection.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These nuns advocated for immigrant rights for 40 years
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — At age 90 and 95, this Catholic nun dynamic duo have been “peacefully and respectfully” fighting for immigrant rights for over 40 years. And they never take no for an answer.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4
